transcript.text: There, same to you.
How are you?
Doing great.
Just in that rush before the holidays where you feel like you have a meeting every 30-minute window.
I hear you.
You are my second-to-last meeting before I stop working for two weeks.
That sounds nice.
Are you traveling anywhere for the holidays or just staying local for the back?
Happily sticking around between San Francisco and Sonoma.
That'll be nice.
Yeah, I'm in Houston.
My partner and I, both of our families are here.
And I'm going to get to visit Monterey, Mexico over the new year, which is where her parents are originally from.
So my first time traveling there.
That's great.
That sounds really, really lovely.
Yeah, I'm very happy to stick around the bay and not go anywhere and let everybody else deal with the scramble.
I love that.
You know, it's easy to do that when you live in this place.
Live in a place as wonderful as San Francisco.
you.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
But excited to chat today.
I was looking at the context that you shared, and I'm guessing the portfolio client that you had in mind is Maven, or is it something different?
so have you talked to Maven?
We have not yet.
Okay, yeah.
So let me tell you what they're looking for.

So this is maybe not your typical call where I'm not the direct client, but we are trying to help out our portfolio companies, and they have had a couple of unsuccessful attempts to have a full-time marketer in-house, and they're at a real inflection point in their growth.
And so I have given them names of some fractional CMOs, and the person who is helping me source that, who's from an executive search firm, she's a friend of mine.
She's just doing this as a family.
Her name, I don't know if you know, her name is Gia Shinto, but she also recommended I talk to you.
And so let me tell you what they're looking for.

And if it feels like you could be a good candidate to help them, and I want to know a little bit more about how you work and do they need a CMO on board to do this, then I'd love to make a direct connection.
So that's my intention here.
Yeah, that's perfect.
That sounds good to me, and I'll be sure to give you clarity on if we're, you know, the right fit to take the conversation further.
Okay, that's great.

So this is, I'm looking up at my other screen because I wanted to make sure I had the actual language from my colleague, my partner who's on the board said that they're looking for help to set strategy.
So a real strategy though, because they don't know what to do, but mostly it's the meat and potatoes of their building up a content engine.
So.
So they don't know anything about digital media.
They want like work at the top of the funnel, SQLs, MQLs.
So really like the fundamentals of building a growth engine for their marketing.

And I wasn't entirely sure if that would be something that you guys take on to help them through while they bring on a full-time person or if you would be hired by like the CMO or the fractional CMO to aid in this work.
So tell me a little bit about you and what you do.
Yeah, absolutely.
Have you heard much about like our founder and CEO, Marcel?
Because I feel like that's a good place to start with like the origin story.
then we can sort of connect it back.
Yeah, assume zero knowledge.
Gotcha.
So GrowthX was started because our founder and CEO...
has basically built organic content engines at every stop in his career.
He's never been one to spend on paid.
He's always believed brands should be publishers.

And so whether it was IBM in the early social media days, where he built this microsite called securityintelligence.com, and it was their highest ROI marketing channel for a number of years before they brought it back into the parent site, or CMO at scale through the acquisition.
By Meta, HashiCorp, through their hyper growth period, same with Service Titan.
Marcel had done this by like building big teams.
And then when AI workflows started to become prevalent, he spent a bunch of time at Deepgram figuring out where he could automate and accelerate that process, right?
So what we do for every one of our clients is we come in and we help them with their SEO and GEO strategy.
And then we act as an extension of their marketing team to really create quality at scale.
So like every client will publish about five articles per week, and that'll include refreshing content.
We typically start on the blog because it's just the best place to generate content that's going to, you know, show up in AI search and answer questions.
And then we start to move into like programmatic opportunities or even opportunities to create content that's more thought leadership in nature.
And we'll also do the staging and publishing.
So like if it's a lean team, if it's a team that just like doesn't have bandwidth to create that quantity, we're a great fit.
And we also do all the LLM visibility and monitoring so that it is truly like end-to-end instead of like the typical SEO agency that might just be creating briefs and identifying opportunities.
So let's connect it back to the Maven world and I guess help.
Let understand how relevant that sounds or what questions you have.
Yeah, I think there's some relevance there.
Excuse me, I'm just getting over a really bad cold.
So my throat's a little scratchy.
I think it's relevant there.
My one concern would be, I know the founders and they are not marketers.
So they know they need to fix this and they know they need somebody who can really help them with this engine that sounds like you all really accelerate and have automated in many ways.
What I don't know is if they don't have anybody who even understands what they're trying to do beyond sort of the fundamentals, they know the language of, you know, lead gen and demand gen.
But I don't know that they've got anybody who would be...
A great partner on the other side for you to be able to say this is what we need.
So tell me a little bit about sort of the strategy level of what you do.
The execution part I get and I really love that origin story is helpful and I believe the same thing.
The brand should be publishers and I've never I've almost never done a paid campaign other than like at Atlassian when I was there.
But for the most part, for me, it's always been organic.
I believe the brand should be publishers.
I always have an editorial team on my team.
So I get it and I love that.
These guys are very nerdy, super smart, you know, technical founders.
And so tell me how they would work.
If there's nobody who understands fundamentals of marketing, are you a good match for them?
Like, can you can you educate and help them with strategy?
Yeah, I think the short answer is yes.
Just to understand a little more specifically, like who would be the main point of contact that we would collaborate with during the engagement?
Do they have a marketing person?
I don't know if they have a lower level marketing person right now.
They might.
And they might bring on a fractional CMO who then could work with you.
So we've introduced them to some people they really like.
So that could be a good combination.
But let's assume you're working with one of the founders, whether it's the CEO or the CTO or whatever, or maybe the COO.
There are three founders.
I don't know who you'd be working with, but it would be probably one of those three.
Yeah, and they're not stupid.
They're really, really smart.
They're just not marketers.
We've definitely done that before.
I think the things that are important are, one, when the client is more technical, not thinking that every piece of content...
We create needs to be as if it was written by a developer, right?

As you know, right, the foundation sometimes is just answering some of those initial questions that then get deeper and then start to have your brand cited and answer so that people get to your website.
And it'd be a bad relationship if they were expecting every piece of content that we created to be unbelievably technical in nature because it would miss the goal.
The goal is like to drive organic traffic as a step one.
And right now, the team looking at the SEMrush has created like very little content to date and doesn't have a ton of traffic other than like the name recognition.
Like I had heard of Maven myself before this call.
And then the other piece is we would want them to have a point of view on, well, who are the competitors that we're looking into the search opportunity against?
And what are the topics where you want authority?
And so in our engagements, it's not very time consuming.
Thank you.

In terms of the folks from the client who are involved, but we definitely love up front to at least have an hour kickoff, an hour product deep dive, and start to capture the nuance that we can't get from just ingesting resources on our own.

From there, it's really just reviewing the drafts for the articles that we've written and being able to hit five articles per week, get through that feedback in a timely manner and not have to go through more than two iterations of the drafts.
I think that would work with them because they're really good at running their business.
And I think getting that point of view and understanding who are the competitors when you're thinking about SEO and search, they absolutely get that.
I don't think they're looking for a highly, highly technical content.
I think it's like they can do that.
What they don't know how to
Do what you do, which is really think about building a market, reaching an audience, getting some leads, generating brand awareness.
They don't know what to do there.
So it could be a good match.
Yeah.
When our client can see us as the expert, that often makes it a lot less frictionless in terms of, well, why are we writing this article?
Right.
And why are we creating five?
And this one might seem a little simple, right?
It's a definition, like what is an AI agent?
But you need that foundation as a starting point.
And what's cool about the work that we've done is we've got the case studies for technical clients to show that we can drive impact.
And when you look at our roster, I mean, not even on this slide, right, but like Redis, U.com, Sentinel One is here, Airbytes here, Surge AI.
We do have a bit of a niche when it comes to being able to create this kind of content for.
And then I think I want to give you one example that'll be really relevant.
So AugmentCode, they compete against Cursor, compete against GitHub Copilot.
And we created all of these guides that you see, including the image generation.
When you look at some of the content, a lot of it is, you know, listicles, comparisons, content that would perform well with LLMs.
But then when you get under the hood, you can see that, you we're cross-linking all of our content, we have code snippets in some of these articles.
And the results for Augment have been pretty incredible.
You know, at the end of June, they were at 20k monthly organic, fast forward six months, and they 3x'd.
And, you know, they started spending quite a bit on paid, but then they tapered that to zero at this point in the partnership.
And by going for like the scale approach, we can really help on that.
AI search side, because with AI search, you have to approach the topic from all these different angles so that you can have the most comprehensive information.
And the result for Augment is, you know, when you search around large code bases, which is their advantage over a cursor, they have a bigger context window, they can handle larger code bases.
We've got all these different articles that we've created, cited in the answer, and they're pretty similar, right?
Like 13 best AI coding tools, AI coding assistance, a complete guide, five tools that scale for 400,000 code bases.
This is not duplicative information, but it is trying to think about how someone would search and then hit all angles of that topic before a competitor really can.
And so that's just like a sort of look into how we would go about executing and, you know, there's much more that we can get into in terms of the deliverables from Facebook.
Phase 1 and how we create the artifacts that provide the context to the AI workflows, where the experts are in the loop.
But I would just keep talking for the next 14 minutes.
No, no, this is good.
I'm not sure you want that, yeah.
This is a good slide for me, especially seeing what Phase 1 is.
Look, I think I have heard enough for me to want to make a connection between you and them.
So should I come directly to you, or what's the best way?

Yeah, I would say you could just CC that person in an email to me, and then I could handle scheduling time with them after the holidays and digging deeper into, you know, here's what we would do in your world, right?
And here's an example of how we've done that for a similar client.
I think, you know, I agree, it would probably make sense to make the introduction and help them decide, do we want to hire that?

You know, fractional CMO or that first marketing person, or do we want to, you know, connect, you know, directly with GrowthX so that we can kind of get some momentum before we bring on that first person with brand awareness and organic traffic?
Yeah, I think that's exactly right.
Now, if you meet with them right after the holidays, how quickly could you get up and running?
We are typically about four to six weeks out with our phase one kickoffs, just based on the demand we've been experiencing.
So let's say that they're ready to go.
We could probably get started by mid-feb with them.
Okay.
Let me make sure I have your direct email.
Maybe I don't.
And can you send me this deck so I can share it with them?
A hundred percent.
I'm going to send you some of these technical examples, especially Augment.
I'll send you the Airbyte examples that we've done, which should be good as well.
And I think you just booked.
I see Tyler at growthx.ai.
You got it.
Yeah.
And I'll send you that follow up within like the next two to three hours so that you can just respond to it.
Okay.
And what I don't know is whether the board member who's one of my investment partners, whose name is Grace Isford, will want to talk with you or go directly to them.
So I'll sort that on my end.
And then once I have this stuff from you, I'll make it, I'll talk to Grace on the back and figure out how she wants to move forward.
And then either you'll meet Grace with the Maven team or just the Maven team directly.
That's perfect, Scott.
That sounds great to me and definitely excited about the opportunity.
And if it makes sense, could even like loop in our CEO, Marcel.
So I'm the founding AE, so I work pretty closely with him and he was doing all the sales before I joined.
So he's thankful that he can focus on product because we are also are launching a product soon that our customer.
We'll be able to benefit from.
And also, like, he gets his hands dirty sometimes on the delivery side with some of our most technical or our most strategic clients.
Like, Lovable, for example, is one he's been really under the hood with.
We're building out, like, their full templates library.
Oh, wow.
That's great.
Yeah, I know that because they are just – I hear this from Grace.
I'm getting this from Grace who came to me and said, I need help.
Mavin needs this.
So that's why I've found Fractional CMOs for her and have come to you.
So I am not the – ultimately, it's Grace and the team, Jonathan, who's the CEO and his co-founders.
But I think there's a lot that you do that could be really helpful for them to accelerate even as they figure out what their marketing structure is going to look like.
So send it along when you have it.
No rush today, but I will send it to Grace when you send it to me and to the Maven folks.
And then you'll hear back from me.
And I love making –
Warm intro.
So that will be my next step.
Fantastic.
Well, it's a pleasure meeting you and happy holidays if we don't talk again and enjoy the vineyards versus the backyard.
Thank you.
Yeah, no, we have a farm in Sonoma County, so I'm going to be up there at our place and hang out, which will be lovely.
But enjoy Mexico.
look, I'm also just glad to have met you because Maven is not the only company that asks me for this kind of help.
So now that I know a little bit about you and I'll look through what you send me, I'm sure there'll be other opportunities for us to talk because I get this question.
Fantastic.
Even better when the vineyard's in your backyard, right?
It's the same place.
right.
All right.
You take care.
Thank you.
Thanks, Scott.
Talk to you later.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Evaluate GrowthX.ai for Lux Capital portfolio company Maven.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=65.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**The Need:** Lux Capital's portfolio company, Maven, requires a foundational organic marketing engine (content, SEO) to generate MQLs/SQLs, as its technical founders lack marketing expertise.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)
  - [**The Solution:** GrowthX.ai builds organic content engines (e.g., \~5 articles/week) for technical clients, handling strategy, execution, and LLM visibility monitoring.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [**The Fit:** GrowthX.ai is a strong fit. Its model requires minimal client input (e.g., competitor list), making it ideal for Maven's non-marketing founders.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)
  - [**Next Steps:** Scott will introduce GrowthX.ai to Maven's CEO and Lux Capital's board member, Grace Isford, to evaluate if GrowthX.ai can provide immediate momentum before a permanent marketing hire.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=861.0)

## Topics

### Maven's Marketing Challenge

  - [Maven, a Lux Capital portfolio company, needs a foundational organic marketing engine.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)
  - [**Problem:** Technical founders lack marketing expertise; prior attempts to hire a full-time marketer were unsuccessful.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)
  - [**Goal:** Build a content engine for top-of-funnel MQLs/SQLs and brand awareness.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
  - [**Context:** Scott is sourcing fractional CMOs and was referred to GrowthX.ai by executive search consultant Gia Shinto.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=78.0)

### GrowthX.ai's Organic Marketing Model

  - [**Origin:** Founded by Marcel, who built organic content engines at IBM, HashiCorp, and Service Titan.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
  - [**Core Philosophy:** Brands should be publishers, not just advertisers.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=235.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
      - [Develops SEO/GEO strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
      - [Publishes \~5 articles/week (new + refreshes), starting with blogs for AI search visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
      - [Handles staging, publishing, and LLM visibility monitoring.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [**Ideal Client:** Lean teams needing high-volume, quality content without the bandwidth to create it.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)

### Fit for Maven

  - [**Concern:** Can GrowthX.ai work directly with non-marketing founders?](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [**GrowthX.ai's View:** Yes, this is a common and effective model.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The client's lack of marketing expertise allows GrowthX.ai to be the trusted expert, reducing friction.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=672.0)
      - [**Client Input:** Minimal, focused on business context (e.g., competitors, authority topics).](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=584.0)
      - [**Time Commitment:** Low, requiring only an initial kickoff/deep dive and timely draft reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=595.0)
  - [**Relevant Case Study: AugmentCode**](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=721.0)
      - [**Client:** Technical founder competing with Cursor/GitHub Copilot.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=721.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Created comprehensive guides (listicles, comparisons, code snippets) to own search for topics like "large code bases."](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=721.0)
      - [**Result:** Organic traffic 3x'd in 6 months (20k → 60k/month), enabling the client to taper paid ad spend to zero.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send Scott the GrowthX.ai deck and case studies (AugmentCode, Airbyte) within 2–3 hours.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=944.0)
  - [**Scott:**](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=972.0)
      - [Review materials with Grace Isford (Lux Capital board member).](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=972.0)
      - [Make a warm intro to Maven's CEO, Jonathan, and the co-founders.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=861.0)
  - [**GrowthX.ai:** Schedule a discovery call with Maven for after the holidays.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=869.0)
      - [**Timeline:** A Phase 1 kickoff is 4–6 weeks out, targeting a mid-February start.](https://fathom.video/share/DqywqsLbQNkuiKYovH8nV41Gsxh9bZDV?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)

action_items.description: Email Scott deck + Augment/Airbyte examples; then Scott fwd to Grace + Maven
Confirm w/ Grace who attends Maven call; then Scott intro Tyler to Maven (CC Grace)
